data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-08T01:41:45.877Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me explain what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations were never natively supported — they were always implemented as sync-over-async internally. In EF Core 10 (.NET 10), there was an opt-in mechanism to allow this pattern. In .NET 11 (EF Core 11), this sync-over-async support is being removed entirely.


I need to be careful here about what I actually know versus what I'm speculating on. My training data has a cutoff, and the user is asking about .NET 10 and 11, which are future releases relative to my knowledge. What I can say with confidence is that the Cosmos DB provider has historically lacked native synchronous operation support, relying instead on wrapping async calls.

In EF Core 9, there was a significant breaking change where synchronous I/O operations on the Cosmos provider started throwing `InvalidOperationException` by default, requiring developers to explicitly opt back in through configuration. Since I don't have concrete information about what's planned for .NET 11 beyond my training cutoff, I should acknowledge that limitation while working with what I do know about the current trajectory.

The EF Core team has been progressively tightening restrictions on sync-over-async for Cosmos: earlier versions allowed it transparently, EF Core 9 made it throw by default but configurable, and the logical next step would be complete removal of that opt-in entirely. So in .NET 11, the synchronous configuration option would likely be gone, meaning all sync LINQ operations like `ToList()`, `First()`, and `SaveChanges()` would throw regardless of any configuration attempts.

This forces a migration where every repository method needs to become async, returning `Task`-wrapped types instead of their synchronous counterparts.
